label: Squarish 1BR / 1 bath — shell is split vertically first (left = private: bedroom + bath, right = public: great room + kitchen), then each half splits horizontally (bedroom front / bath rear on the left; great room front / kitchen rear on the right). Bath reached only via the kitchen, never through the bedroom.

adjacencies.note: bedroom (front) and bath (rear) stack vertically along the left wall — shared wall only, no door between them. Keeps the bath from being accessible only through the bedroom, per the locked no-bedroom-through-bath / no-bath-through-bedroom rule.

bedroom's only door opens east into the great_room — the one occupant's sole route in and out. Sized 0.9 m (vs the 0.8 m generic bedroom_to_public floor) to give a slightly more generous door for the single, larger bedroom this topology carries.

bath's only door opens onto the kitchen — bath and kitchen sit rear-left/rear-right across the straight zone-split seam, sharing the plumbing wall AND the door. Bath never touches great_room (they sit in diagonal quadrants), so it's reached via the kitchen — still guest-accessible without walking through the bedroom.

great_room + kitchen form the open LDK (gr style). counter_divider: a 0.6 m dining counter (breakfast bar) renders along this open edge on the kitchen side, with 2 stools on the great_room side and a >= 0.9 m walk-through gap — dining happens at the counter in this compact plan. min_shared_wall_m raised 1.5 -> 2.1 (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass) so the counter always fits the realized edge.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: ultra_compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: At true 5x5 m envelopes (~25 sqm), the bedroom's top priority weight lets it balloon past 11 sqm, crowding kitchen down to its bare hard minimum and leaving no room for great_room to absorb any freed space. Capping bedroom at 9.5 sqm (close to the reference '25 Sqm' tiny-house layout's 8.75 sqm bedroom) lets snap_gaps() redistribute the freed area into great_room instead. This cap is WRONG for larger lots (it blocked the 40-90 sqm range entirely when tested at buildable_area_lt_sqm=100) so it's scoped to only this ultra-compact tier, below the broader compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr profile's 100 sqm threshold.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: 1BR footprints run smaller than the 2BR catalog's compact band, so the threshold drops to 40 sqm buildable -- widened to 100 sqm (2026-07-16) so the common_bath max_area_sqm cap covers the whole practical 1BR range (matches the 2BR baths_ds_ld precedent's 100 sqm threshold). Kitchen floors at 1.8 m least-dim for a usable single-run galley; bath floors at 2.8 sqm and caps at 4.5 sqm (matches the room_catalog's soft preferred_area_sqm upper bound) -- without the cap, snap_gaps() was dumping leftover envelope area into the bath uncapped (e.g. 10.8 sqm on a 10x10 m lot, ~30% of the whole house) since no other room absorbed the gap on some lot sizes. Below 30 sqm, the ultra_compact_sq_side_split_bath_gr profile above matches first and additionally caps bedroom_standard tighter (9.5 sqm). bedroom_standard set_max_area_sqm=20.0 (2026-07-16): restores the headroom this room had when it was typed master_bedroom (catalog ceiling 20.0 vs bedroom_standard's 12.0). Uses set_max_area_sqm -- the cap-REPLACING knob added for exactly this case -- because plain max_area_sqm can only lower a type's catalog ceiling, never raise it.

notes: First 1BR entry in the squarish catalog. Floor band: roughly 24-36 sqm buildable —
the natural footprint for a single-occupant or couple's starter home on a 60-72 sqm
lot (typical socialized/economic PH subdivision cut) after setbacks.
Program: 1 bedroom, 1 common bath (no ensuite by design — a single ensuite bath
would force guests to walk through the bedroom to use it, which the rest of the
catalog's access rules already forbid for multi-bedroom units and which is just as
poor practice here). The bath instead doors off the kitchen (per the reference
'25 Sqm' plan -- bath and great_room sit in diagonal quadrants and never touch),
so it works for the occupant and visitors alike without crossing the bedroom.
Layout intent (canonical form — brief.carport_side='right'):
Front: [bedroom | great_room          ]
Rear:  [bath     | kitchen             ]
Bedroom sits front-left with its only door opening onto the great_room. Bath sits
directly behind it at rear-left, sharing its plumbing wall AND its door with the
kitchen (rear-right). This is the smallest-footprint pattern in the 1BR catalog —
a clean 2x2 room grid split by one straight vertical wall, no hallway, since a
single bedroom and single bath don't need one to avoid cross-traffic.
Carport: modest 2.5 x 5.0 m single bay (vs. the 2BR catalog's 3.0 x 6.0 m) — sized
for a compact car or motorcycle, reflecting the smaller lot this unit typically
sits on. Declared as a setback element only; no building_void needed unless the
specific lot's side setback is narrower than 2.5 m, in which case a void follows
the same pattern as the 2BR sq topology.
When brief.carport_side='left', the runner mirrors the topology so bedroom/bath
end up front-right/rear-right and kitchen sits rear-left.
BUGFIX (2026-07-16): left_anchored originally listed both 'bedroom' and 'bath',
forcing both to the same left wall AND (via their separate required adjacencies
to great_room and kitchen) implicitly forcing bedroom and bath to the exact same
width -- combined with the solver's hardcoded kitchen/great_room front-to-rear
stacking rule, this over-constrained the 4-room grid so no layout existed at ANY
lot size (confirmed via CP-SAT infeasibility across a full sweep, 4-121 sqm floor).
Fixed by dropping 'bath' from left_anchored -- bath's position is now driven
purely by its own adjacencies (bath_door to great, wet_core to kitchen), same
pattern the working 2BR squarish sibling already uses for its common bath.
(Superseded by the STRAIGHT-CUT redesign below, which reinstates the double
left-anchor now that the true root blocker is fixed.)
REDESIGN (2026-07-16): matched to a reference '25 Sqm' tiny-house floor plan
(square-topology.png) at a true 5x5 m buildable envelope (9x9 m lot, standard
2 m setbacks -- NOT 1m setbacks, which fail PD 1096's 2.0 m window-yard minimum
on the bedroom/bath). In that reference bath never reaches great_room's wall,
so bath's door goes through kitchen exclusively; the old 'bath_access'
door_host_group (default=great_room, alternate=kitchen) required bath to always
reach great_room, which made 5x5 infeasible. Fixed by removing the bath<->great
adjacency entirely and making bath<->kitchen kind='bath_door' directly.
STRAIGHT-CUT (2026-07-16): the envelope is now split by ONE straight vertical
wall -- private column left (bedroom front / bath rear), public column right
(great_room front / kitchen rear) -- via zone_split + match_widths
[bedroom,bath] and [great,kitchen] + double left/right anchors. The true
blocker for this layout was the solver's hard 'private total >= public total'
area rule: the reference plan is public-heavy (bedroom 8.75 vs LDK 12.5 sqm),
which no 1BR program can satisfy -- a single bedroom can't outweigh the LDK
the way a multi-bedroom wing does. Disabled per-topology with the new
private_area_floor: false flag (also skips the 55/45 soft bias). kitchen is
mechanical_vent (its only exterior wall is the rear back-door wall). The
compact profile's bedroom_standard set_max_area_sqm=20.0 uses the new
cap-REPLACING knob (plain max_area_sqm can only lower the catalog's 12.0
ceiling for this type, never raise it).
At 9x9 m (5x5 env): bedroom 2.6x3.5=9.1, bath 2.6x1.5=3.9, great 2.4x2.5=6.0,
kitchen 2.4x2.5=6.0 -- 25.0/25 sqm tiled, zero snaps, COMPLIANT, 1 soft warning
(intended bath->kitchen door). Sweep-verified square-lot range: 8.5x8.5 to
12x12 m (64 sqm floor -- already past the sensible 1BR ceiling; the 2BR band
starts at 45 sqm floor per floor-area-per-br, so larger lots should be offered
2BR instead). 12.5x12.5 is achievable if ever needed by adding aspect_overrides
{"bath": 2.5} (verified) -- the binding constraint there is bath's 2:1 aspect
cap limiting the private column's width.
